source: Sed, Awk & Grep - Awk command: if/for/while, associative arrays, built-in functions, and getline

questions.why: A C-style three-part for loop; for (i in array) also iterates array keys.

questions.q: What do break and continue do inside a loop?
options: break exits the loop; continue skips to the next iteration

questions.q: What kind of arrays does awk have?
options: Associative -- the subscript (index) can be a number OR a string
questions.why: awk arrays are associative: array[index]=value with numeric or string keys; for (i in a) iterates the keys.

questions.q: What does the idiom a[$1]++ do (and !a[$1]++)?
options: a[$1]++ counts occurrences of each value of field 1; !a[$1]++ prints only the first occurrence (dedupe)
questions.why: a[$1]++ tallies per key; !a[$1]++ is the classic keep-first-occurrence dedupe.

questions.q: Which built-in function globally replaces text (like sed's s///g)?
options: gsub(r,s) -- gsub is global, sub(r,s) replaces only the first match
questions.why: gsub does a global replace; sub replaces the first match only.

options: Reads the next input record (into $0, or into a named variable)
questions.why: getline advances the input, e.g. to peek at or process the following line.
